name: lookback_boundary_exactly_19
description: Exactly 19 days of data — one below the 20-day lookback minimum. The function's documented short-circuit (len(valid_dates) < 20 -> return 0.0) must fire even though the underlying returns are highly dispersed. If a wrong impl computed stdev on 19 samples instead of clamping, it would return ~3.0 instead of 0.0 — this fixture catches that.
derivation: len(valid_dates) = 19 < 20 -> short-circuit returns 0.0 regardless of the input dispersion. Were the short-circuit removed, np.std of 19 alternating +/-3.0 samples would be ~2.997 (NOT 3.0 because 19 is odd) — confirms test would fail on a missing clamp.
